articles.title: Trump 'not satisfied' with new Iran proposal amid ceasefire stalemate
articles.description: US President Donald Trump said Friday he was "not satisfied" with a new Iranian negotiating proposal, as peace talks remain frozen despite a weeks-long ceasefire. "At this moment I'm not satisfied with what they're offering," Trump told reporters, blaming stalled talks on "tremendous discord" within Iran's leadership.

articles.title: Trump calls critics of Iran war 'treasonous', says US is winning
articles.description: US President Donald Trump labelled critics of the Iran conflict 'treasonous,' asserting the US is winning. He claimed Iran's military is 'decimated' and expressed dissatisfaction with their latest proposal for a deal to end the conflict.

articles.title: Iran's IRGC announces new rules for control over Arabian Gulf waters
articles.description: Iran's IRGC announced 'new rules' to tighten control over the Arabian Gulf and Strait of Hormuz. The move aims to control 2,000 km of coastline amid escalating tensions with the US, which recently imposed a naval blockade.

articles.title: No zoom for marriage fixes: Andhra Pradesh High Court rejects US-based man’s plea for virtual reconciliation
articles.description: Dismissing US based man's plea for virtual appearance in matrimonial dispute, the Andhra Pradesh High Court stated that at that stage of reconciliation, until it fails, video conferencing is not permissible for such purpose.

articles.title: US Treasury Secretary Bessent calls Iran leadership 'rats in a sewer pipe', reaffirms blockade
articles.description: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent criticizes Iran’s leadership, warns of continued Strait of Hormuz blockade, and signals potential sanctions on shipping companies.

articles.title: West Bengal polls: Lockdown in strongroom areas after TMC, BJP clash
articles.description: Kolkata police have imposed strict prohibitory orders around EVM strongrooms following Trinamool's vote tampering claims and clashes with BJP workers. Security has been heightened with additional forces and sealed roads. The Election Commission vows to punish any disruption, emphasizing robust security at all counting centers statewide to ensure a peaceful electoral process.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Nicolas Cage: 'The end of the world is on people's minds. We have the power to destroy or...' – inspiring lessons on responsibility, human power and how our choices shape the world by Oscar-winning actor of Leaving Las Vegas
articles.description: Quote of the day by Nicolas Cage: Nicolas Cage emphasizes that humans possess immense power to shape the world, capable of both destruction and salvation. He highlights that our daily choices, not just grand actions, influence global outcomes. The actor's quote urges introspection on how we wield this power and responsibility, stressing conscious action over fear of the future.

BJP dismissed these claims as tactics by a party anticipating defeat. TMC representatives reported seeing individuals inside sealed rooms without party agents present, fueling suspicion ahead of counting day.

articles.title: France Breaks With Washington, Demanding End to US Blockade Of Strait Of Hormuz
articles.description: The Western alliance is fracturing at the world’s most critical chokepoint. French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot has officially condemned the U.S. blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, calling it an illegal act that is punishing the global economy. As fuel prices skyrocket, France and the UK are moving to establish their own "neutral" naval mission—independent of President Trump’s command. With Washington telling Europe to "STAY AWAY," Paris is choosing strategic autonomy over a war it says it did not approve.

articles.title: West Asia Crisis Tagged As War': Govt Allows Force Majeure, Grants Relief On Contracts
articles.description: Govt allows contractors to invoke Force Majeure due to the ongoing West Asia situation.

articles.title: Video: Row erupts over 'intoxicated' Bhagwant Mann. BJP, Congress attack Punjab CM, say 'same man who promises drug-free state'
articles.description: Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann is facing sharp criticism from leaders of the Bharatiya Janata Party and Indian National Congress over allegations that he appeared intoxicated in the Assembly. Opposition parties have described the incident as “shameful” and “disgraceful”, raising serious questions about his conduct and the governance of the state. The controversy has intensified even as Mann secured a confidence motion in the House.

articles.title: Rally, public meeting mark May Day celebrations in Puducherry
articles.description: May Day celebrations in Puducherry featured a rally by trade unions, highlighting workers' rights and solidarity.

articles.description: Repolling to be held at 15 booths in West Bengal tomorrow, ECI said.

articles.description: Repoll has been ordered by the EC at 15 booths across South 24 Parganas, including 11 in Magrahat Paschim and four in Diamond Harbour.

articles.title: The Battle for Truth: How Beijing Rewrote the COVID-19 Narrative
articles.description: Since the onset of COVID-19 in Wuhan, the Chinese Communist Party has engaged in a campaign to control the narrative surrounding the pandemic. Utilising propaganda and censorship, China aimed to project an image of effective governance while suppressing dissent.

articles.title: EC's big move before results: Repoll ordered at 15 Bengal booths, voting on May 2
articles.description: Repolling has been ordered at 15 polling stations in West Bengal on May 2 by the Election Commission, as BJP–TMC tensions mount over allegations of irregularities before the final election results.

articles.description: Tarun Joshi assumes role as Future City Police Commissioner, focusing on infrastructure, law enforcement, and community trust.

articles.title: Why Poll Body Ordered Re-Elections In West Bengal's Magrahat Paschim And Diamond Harbour Seats
articles.description: The original polling held on April 29 at these booths has been declared void under Section 58(2) of the Representation of the People Act, 1951

articles.title: EC orders repolling in 15 polling booths in West Bengal on Saturday
articles.description: The Election Commission on Friday ordered repolling on May 2 in 15 polling stations in West Bengal, where voting was held on April 29 in the second phase of the assembly elections. In a letter to the Chief Electoral Officer of Bengal, the Election Commission (EC) said the repolling has been ordered based on inputs received from the state poll machinery and voting will be held from 7 am to 6 pm. While 11 polling stations are in the Magarhat Paschim assembly constituency, four are in Diamond Harbour. A report about complaints relating to the Falta constituency is awaited. A large number of complaints was received from the constituency. This is the first time that repolling has been ordered in the present set of assembly polls in West Bengal, Assam, Tamil Nadu, Kerala and Puducherry. No repolling was recommended in the first phase of the West Bengal polls held on April 23.

articles.title: Mallikarjun Kharge breaks silence amid leadership change buzz in Karnataka: 'No date is fixed'
articles.description: The Congress president said that any decision regarding leadership would be taken collectively by the party's high command after due consultation.

articles.title: Supreme Court Grants Anticipatory Bail to Pawan Khera, Sets Aside Guwahati HC Order
articles.description: The Supreme Court of India has granted anticipatory bail to Congress leader Pawan Khera in a case registered by Assam Police. Setting aside the Gauhati High Court order, the apex court observed that the allegations appear to carry political overtones and stressed that personal liberty cannot be lightly curtailed.The case stems from remarks made by Khera regarding Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma’s wife, leading to charges of defamation, forgery and criminal conspiracy.Earlier, Khera had approached the Telangana High Court for transit bail before moving the Gauhati High Court, which rejected his plea citing the need for custodial interrogation. Challenging that decision, Khera moved the Supreme Court, which has now granted him protection from arrest.The matter remains under investigation.

articles.title: AAP's bid to ‘settle score’ in Punjab? Inside big raid after Rajinder Gupta’s BJP switch
articles.description: AAP said that the allegations of "political vendetta" were baseless and called the Trident raid a routine exercise.

articles.title: 'Never even been to the location': JP Morgan executive Lorna Hajdini responds to sex harassment claims by 'brown boy Indian' Chirayu Rana
articles.description: JP Morgan executive Lorna Hajdini denies allegations of sexual harassment and abuse made by a junior employee in a lawsuit. The company's internal investigation found no evidence to support the claims, stating the complainant refused to cooperate. The lawsuit alleges coercive sexual acts, drugging, and professional retaliation.

articles.title: WB Minister Shashi Panja slams ECI for restricting strong room footage
articles.description: WB Minister Shashi Panja accused the ECI of restricting strong room footage access amidst TMC protests over alleged ballot tampering. CM Mamata Banerjee also alleged EVM tampering and said her party will fight any attempts to 'steal the counting'.

articles.title: TMC raises alarm over strong room security, alleges BJP interference
articles.description: TMC's Saugata Roy alleged irregularities in strong room security and BJP's interference ahead of vote counting. CM Mamata Banerjee also visited a strong room, warning against any attempts to tamper with EVMs and the counting process.

articles.title: Yogi Adityanath launches labour welfare schemes on International Labour Day
articles.description: UP CM Yogi Adityanath marked International Labour Day by inaugurating 'Shramveer Gaurav Samaroh 2026' and launching new welfare schemes. He hailed workers as the architects of a new India, while BSP chief Mayawati raised concerns over their conditions.

articles.title: Chhattisgarh High Court refuses to quash Army sepoy’s case over abusive WhatsApp post on former CM Bhupesh Baghel
articles.description: The counsel for the petitioner, submitted that the alleged statement was made under severe mental stress.

articles.title: Harbhajan Singh moves Punjab HC after AAP govt withdraws security
articles.description: Rajya Sabha MP Harbhajan Singh has moved the Punjab and Haryana High Court after the Punjab Govt withdrew his security cover. The cricketer-turned-politician, who recently joined the BJP from AAP, has sought immediate reinstatement of his protection.
